issue_title: Try to sign up with an existing account throws no error

reasoning_trace.finding: Initial repository-wide search was too broad and included generated analysis artifacts; narrowed subsequent searches to Simplenote/src/main.

Found signup-related classes: SignupFragment.java, ConfirmationFragment.java, MagicLinkableFragment.kt, SignInFragment.kt, SimplenoteSignupActivity.java, NewCredentialsActivity.kt, and existing-account strings.
Manifest declares .authentication.SimplenoteSignupActivity at lines 173-175 and NewCredentialsActivity at lines 177-178; signup activity is an explicit auth entry point.
buttonSignupClicked() starts SimplenoteSignupActivity without KEY_IS_LOGIN, so the sign-up route enters SimplenoteSignupActivity in sign-up mode.
onCreate() computes isSignUp from KEY_IS_LOGIN, and createFragment(true) returns new SignupFragment(), confirming SignupFragment is the component for the reported steps.
The signup screen contains input_email and button with text @string/simperium_button_signup; SignupFragment wires these views.
listenToSignupClick() calls signupUser(emailEditText.getText().toString()) when network is available; signupUser enqueues an OkHttp request to account/request-signup.
buildCall() posts JSON {"username": email} to https://app.simplenote.com/account/request-signup with Accept-Language.

onFailure() shows a generic signup error, but onResponse() hides progress, hides keyboard, and always calls showConfirmationScreen(email) without checking response.isSuccessful(), response.code(), or response body.
ConfirmationFragment.newInstance(email, true) stores isSignUp=true; initEmailConfirmation() chooses R.string.email_confirmation_text for signup and renders the supplied email.
The confirmation layout displays an email icon and email_confirmation_text; tools text matches the reported screen saying an email was sent.
Found dialog_message_signup_error and simperium_dialog_message_signup_existing; the app already has copy for an existing Simplenote account.

Auth listener handles FailureType.EXISTING_ACCOUNT by calling showDialogErrorExistingAccount(), which displays simperium_dialog_message_signup_existing; this confirms expected behavior exists elsewhere.
Magic-link repository checks response.isSuccessful and returns MagicLinkError on unsuccessful HTTP responses, unlike SignupFragment.
Magic-link request view model maps MagicLinkError to an error UI state instead of showing confirmation, further eliminating the magic-link path as the source of this signup symptom.
MagicLinkableFragment can also show ConfirmationFragment for signup, but this shared path is invoked by subclasses; the concrete reported email-only sign-up path is SignupFragment.
SignInFragment is login-only and uses RequestMagicLinkViewModel; it is not the sign-up-with-existing-email path.

reasoning_trace.target: SignupFragment.onResponse unconditionally treats any HTTP response from account/request-signup as signup success.
reasoning_trace.finding: If the backend rejects an existing email with an HTTP response rather than a transport failure, OkHttp calls onResponse; the code ignores the error status/body and navigates to the confirmation screen.
ConfirmationFragment only renders the state passed to it; it does not decide whether signup succeeded, so it is not the root fault.
The activity correctly selects SignupFragment for sign-up; it does not process server responses.
This path correctly displays an existing-account error and is evidence of the missing branch in SignupFragment, not the fault.
This exact statement causes the observed false success screen because it is executed for every HTTP response, including existing-account rejection responses.

fault_candidates.reasoning: OkHttp invokes onResponse for HTTP responses even when the server reports an application-level failure such as an existing account. This callback ignores response.isSuccessful(), response.code(), and response body, then always calls showConfirmationScreen(email), which renders the 'We've sent an email' screen. That exactly matches the report: signup with an already registered email fails server-side but the app displays a success confirmation and no email arrives.

final_answer.causal_chain: User taps sign up in SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity, which starts SimplenoteSignupActivity. SimplenoteSignupActivity creates SignupFragment. SignupFragment.listenToSignupClick() calls signupUser(email). signupUser() posts to account/request-signup and enqueues buildCallback(email). For an email already associated with an account, the server returns an HTTP response indicating signup cannot proceed. SignupFragment.onResponse() does not inspect the HTTP status or error payload; it hides progress and executes showConfirmationScreen(email). showConfirmationScreen() creates ConfirmationFragment with isSignUp=true, and ConfirmationFragment displays R.string.email_confirmation_text saying an email was sent. Since the server did not actually create a signup email for the existing account, the user sees a false success screen and receives no email.
final_answer.fix_hint: In onResponse(), check response.isSuccessful()/response.code() and route existing-account or other failure responses to an error dialog, using simperium_dialog_message_signup_existing where appropriate, instead of unconditionally calling showConfirmationScreen(email).
